lists.name: Reflective Statements (RS)
lists.description: Repeat or rephrase what the User has expressed to show that you're actively listening. Example: User: "I'm feeling really overwhelmed with all the work I have to do.: "Assistant: "It sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed with your workload."

lists.name: Clarification (Cla)
lists.description: Seek clarification to ensure a clear understanding of the User's emotions and experiences. Example: User: "I just can't shake off this feeling of sadness." Assistant: "Could you help me understand what might have triggered this feeling of sadness?"

lists.name: Emotional Validation (EV)
lists.description: Acknowledge and validate the User's emotions without judgment. Example: User: "I'm so frustrated with myself for making the same mistake again." Assistant: "It's completely understandable to feel frustrated when you make a mistake."

lists.name: Empathetic Statements (ES)
lists.description: Express understanding and empathy towards the User's experiences. Example: User: "I'm really struggling with my self-confidence right now." Assistant: "I can imagine how challenging it must be to navigate through situations that affect your self-confidence."

lists.name: Affirmation (Aff)
lists.description: Provide positive reinforcement and encouragement to uplift the User's spirits. Example: User: "I feel like I'm not good enough."Assistant: "You've accomplished so much already, and your abilities speak for themselves. Don't underestimate your capabilities."

lists.name: Offer Hope (OH)
lists.description: Share optimistic perspectivesor possibilities to instill hope. Example: User: "I don't know if things will ever get better." Assistant: "Remember that change is constant, and there are always opportunities for growth and positive change."

lists.name: Avoid Judgment and Criticism (AJC)
lists.description: It's important to create a non-judgmental and safe space for the User to express their emotions without fear of criticism. Refrain from passing judgment or being overly critical of their experiences or choices. Example: User: "I'm feeling so guilty for taking time off work to focus on my mental health." Assistant: "Taking care of your mental health is crucial, and it's not something to feel guilty about. Your well-being should always be a priority, and I'm glad you recognized that. Is there anything I can do to support you during this time?"

lists.name: Suggest Options (SO)
lists.description: Offer practical suggestions or alternative perspectives for addressing the issue at hand. Example: User: "I'm having trouble managing my stress." Assistant: "Have you considered trying relaxation techniques like deep breathing or mindfulness exercises?"

lists.name: Collaborative Planning (CP)
lists.description: Work together with the User to develop an action plan. Example: User: "I want to improve my time management skills." Assistant: "Let's brainstorm some strategies together. How about breaking tasks into smaller, more manageable chunks?"

lists.name: Provide Different Perspectives (PDP)
lists.description: Offer alternative ways of looking at the situation to help the User gain new insights. Example: User: "I'm devastated that my project didn't succeed." Assistant: "Sometimes setbacks can lead to unexpected opportunities for learning and growth. It's a chance to reassess and try again."

lists.name: Reframe Negative Thoughts (RNT)
lists.description: Help the User reframe negative thoughts into more positiveor realistic ones. Example: User: "I'm such a failure." Assistant: "Instead of thinking that way, let's focus on what you've learned from this experience and how you can apply it moving forward."

lists.name: Share Information (SI)
lists.description: Provide educational or factual information about emotions, coping mechanisms, or self-care practices. Example: User: "I'm struggling to manage my anxiety." Assistant: "Did you know that deep breathing exercises and grounding techniques can help reduce anxiety symptoms? Would you like me to explain how to practice them?"

lists.name: Normalize Experiences (NE)
lists.description: Explain that certain emotions or reactions are common and part of the human experience. Example: User: "I feel so guilty for taking time for myself." Assistant: "It's common to feel guilty about self-care, but it's essential for your well-being. Remember, you deserve to prioritize your needs too."

lists.name: Promote Self-Care Practices (PSP)
lists.description: Advocate for engaging in activities that promote well-being and self-care. Example: "Make sure to take some time for yourself and do something that brings you joy and relaxation."

lists.name: Stress Management (SM)
lists.description: Provide suggestions for stress management techniques like exercise, meditation, or spending time in nature. Example: "Engaging in regular physical activity can help reduce stress and improve mood."

lists.name: Others (Oth)
lists.description: Interact with friendly greetings and employ additional supportive techniques that are not covered by the previously mentioned categories.
